title: News Coverage Highlights Samsung SK Hynix Leveraged ETFs Backlash
description: Recent reports from CNBC, Bloomberg, and Reuters discuss the impact of market selloffs on leveraged ETFs involving Samsung and SK Hynix.

sources.title: 'Give me my money back': South Korean traders' leveraged SK Hynix, Samsung bets unravel after selloff
Korea President’s Stock Dream Bumps Into Leveraged ETF Backlash
Breakingviews - Seoul AI ETF howler morphs into zombie volatility

sections.heading: Trader Reactions to Market Selloff
paragraphs: According to CNBC, South Korean traders' leveraged bets on SK Hynix and Samsung have unraveled following a selloff. The report highlights a sentiment among traders demanding their money back.

sections.heading: Political Context and ETF Backlash
paragraphs: Bloomberg.com reports that the South Korean President's aspirations for the stock market have collided with a backlash against leveraged ETFs.

sections.heading: Market Analysis and Volatility
paragraphs: A commentary by Reuters describes a situation involving a Seoul AI ETF as a 'howler' that has morphed into 'zombie volatility'.

sections.heading: Search Trend Data
paragraphs: Google Trends data indicates a search volume lower bound of 200 for the term 'samsung sk hynix leveraged etfs' in the US region. The trend feed was published on July 20, 2026.

faq.q: What sources are covering the Samsung and SK Hynix ETF situation?
faq.a: CNBC, Bloomberg.com, and Reuters have all published reports regarding the issues surrounding leveraged ETFs linked to these companies.

faq.q: What does CNBC say about the traders?
faq.a: According to CNBC, South Korean traders have seen their leveraged bets on SK Hynix and Samsung unravel after a market selloff.

faq.q: How is the situation described by Reuters?
faq.a: According to Reuters, a Seoul AI ETF issue is described as a 'howler' that has morphed into 'zombie volatility'.
